Operation 'resumeMonitor' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_context, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original summary is too generic. Description lacks critical details about what happens when resumed (checks resume at configured interval), what is returned (updated monitor object), and the state change effect. Added context about permissions, return value, and side effects for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description: Resume a previously paused uptime monitor.
data.description_suggestion: Resumes a previously paused uptime monitor, restoring its monitoring checks. The monitor will resume checking at its configured interval. Requires the monitor ID and appropriate API key permissions. Returns the updated monitor object with status reflecting the resumed state. This operation changes the monitor's state from paused to active.
data.reasoning: Original summary is too generic. Description lacks critical details about what happens when resumed (checks resume at configured interval), what is returned (updated monitor object), and the state change effect. Added context about permissions, return value, and side effects for better AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'listIncidents' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details. Original summary lacks specificity about pagination. Description is too minimal - doesn't explain what parameters are available (filtering, sorting), what incident data is returned, or permission requirements. Enhanced version provides context about pagination support, return data structure, and access control for improved AI agent interpretability.

data.current_description: Get a paginated list of uptime incidents.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a paginated list of uptime incidents across monitored resources. Supports filtering and sorting via query parameters. Returns incident objects containing details such as status, affected monitors, duration, and timestamps. Pagination metadata is included to navigate through results. Requires appropriate API key permissions to view incidents.
data.reasoning: Original summary lacks specificity about pagination. Description is too minimal - doesn't explain what parameters are available (filtering, sorting), what incident data is returned, or permission requirements. Enhanced version provides context about pagination support, return data structure, and access control for improved AI agent interpretability.

message: Operation 'listMonitors' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Original summary is too generic. Description mentions the project header requirement but lacks details about pagination parameters, filtering/sorting capabilities, and the response structure. Enhanced version clarifies input requirements, available parameters, and expected output format for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description: Get a paginated list of uptime monitors. Organization-scoped API keys require a project selection header.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve paginated list of uptime monitors
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a paginated list of uptime monitors for the specified project. Organization-scoped API keys require the X-Project-Id header to specify which project's monitors to list. Supports filtering and sorting via query parameters. Returns a MonitorListResponse containing monitor objects and pagination metadata.
data.reasoning: Original summary is too generic. Description mentions the project header requirement but lacks details about pagination parameters, filtering/sorting capabilities, and the response structure. Enhanced version clarifies input requirements, available parameters, and expected output format for better AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'createMonitor' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description is minimal and lacks critical details. Enhanced version specifies the request schema structure, authentication requirements, return value, and important side effect (immediate activation). This helps AI agents understand what configuration is needed and what to expect after creation.

data.description_suggestion: Creates a new uptime monitor with the specified configuration. Accepts a MonitorCreateRequest containing monitor name, URL, check interval, request configuration, and success assertions. Organization-scoped API keys require the X-Project-Id header. Returns the created Monitor object with a generated ID and initial status. The monitor will begin checking immediately upon creation.
data.reasoning: Original description is minimal and lacks critical details. Enhanced version specifies the request schema structure, authentication requirements, return value, and important side effect (immediate activation). This helps AI agents understand what configuration is needed and what to expect after creation.

message: Operation 'updateMonitor' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description is too minimal. Enhanced version clarifies what fields can be updated, authentication requirements, return value, and the immediate effect of changes. This enables AI agents to understand the scope of modifications and their impact on monitoring.

data.description_suggestion: Updates an existing uptime monitor's configuration. Accepts a MonitorCreateRequest with updated monitor settings such as name, URL, check interval, request configuration, and success assertions. Organization-scoped API keys require the X-Project-Id header. Returns the updated Monitor object. Changes take effect immediately and affect ongoing monitoring.
data.reasoning: Original description is too minimal. Enhanced version clarifies what fields can be updated, authentication requirements, return value, and the immediate effect of changes. This enables AI agents to understand the scope of modifications and their impact on monitoring.

message: Operation 'deleteMonitor' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description lacks important context about permanence and side effects. Enhanced version clarifies that deletion is permanent, stops monitoring, specifies the response code, and notes that historical data is preserved. This helps AI agents understand the consequences of this destructive operation.

data.description_suggestion: Permanently deletes an uptime monitor and stops all associated checks. Organization-scoped API keys require the X-Project-Id header. Returns a 204 No Content response on successful deletion. This action cannot be undone - all historical data for the monitor is retained but the monitor will no longer perform checks.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks important context about permanence and side effects. Enhanced version clarifies that deletion is permanent, stops monitoring, specifies the response code, and notes that historical data is preserved. This helps AI agents understand the consequences of this destructive operation.

message: Operation 'pauseMonitor' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is clear and actionable. Description explains the effect but lacks details about authentication requirements, return value, and what happens to historical data. Enhanced version provides complete information for AI agents to understand the operation's scope and reversibility.

data.current_description: Pause an active uptime monitor. It will stop being checked until resumed.
data.description_suggestion: Pauses an active uptime monitor, temporarily halting all checks without deleting the monitor configuration. Organization-scoped API keys require the X-Project-Id header. Returns the updated Monitor object with status set to 'paused'. The monitor can be resumed later to continue checking. Historical data is preserved during the pause.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and actionable. Description explains the effect but lacks details about authentication requirements, return value, and what happens to historical data. Enhanced version provides complete information for AI agents to understand the operation's scope and reversibility.

data.current_description: Resume a previously paused uptime monitor.
data.description_suggestion: Resumes a previously paused uptime monitor, restoring its monitoring checks. The monitor will resume checking at its configured interval. Requires the monitor ID and appropriate API key permissions. Returns the updated monitor object with status reflecting the resumed state. This operation changes the monitor's state from paused to active.
